bibliographic_data.title: Adaptable Multi-Domain Language Model for Transformer ASR
bibliographic_data.abstract: We propose an adapter based multi-domain Transformer based language model (LM) for Transformer ASR. The model consists of a big size common LM and small size adapters. The model can perform multi-domain adaptation with only the small size adapters and its related layers. The proposed model can reuse the full fine-tuned LM which is fine-tuned using all layers of an original model. The proposed LM can be expanded to new domains by adding about 2% of parameters for a first domain and 13% parameters for after second domain. The proposed model is also effective in reducing the model maintenance cost because it is possible to omit the costly and time-consuming common LM pre-training process. Using proposed adapter based approach, we observed that a general LM with adapter can outperform a dedicated music domain LM in terms of word error rate (WER).

core_content.novelty_claim: The model proposes an adapter-based multi-domain Transformer language model for ASR, which can be adapted to new domains with minimal parameter additions.
core_content.key_methodology: The method involves using a large common LM and small size adapters. The adapters are added in front of each batch normalization layer in series or parallel manner. This allows the model to perform multi-domain adaptation efficiently.
outcomes: The proposed model can reuse the full fine-tuned LM which is fine-tuned using all layers of an original model.
The model can be expanded to new domains by adding about 2% of parameters for a first domain and 13% parameters for after second domain.
core_content.full_text_markdown: In this paper, the authors propose an adapter-based multi-domain Transformer language model (LM) for ASR. The model consists of a large common LM and small size adapters that can be added to perform multi-domain adaptation with minimal parameter additions. This approach allows the model to reuse the full fine-tuned LM which is fine-tuned using all layers of an original model. The proposed model can be expanded to new domains by adding about 2% of parameters for a first domain and 13% parameters for after second domain. Additionally, this method reduces the maintenance cost as it omits the costly and time-consuming common LM pre-training process. Experimental results show that using the adapter-based approach, a general LM with adapters can outperform a dedicated music domain LM in terms of word error rate (WER).
